description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: configure, test, and deploy, using the docker:stable image (with docker:dind service) for Docker jobs and node:lts for Node.js jobs. In the configure stage add a Build and Deploy CI image job that runs docker build --tag "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CONTAINER_TAG}" --target build ., then echo "${CI_DEPLOY_PASSWORD}" | docker login -u "${CI_DEPLOY_USER}" --password-stdin "${CI_REGISTRY}", and finally docker push "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CONTAINER_TAG}" (CONTAINER_TAG is set to ci). Also in configure add a Check Semantic Version job that runs only on the master branch, executes ./scripts/check_semantic_versioning.sh, and defines a GITLAB_MERGE_REQUEST_API variable pointing to the merge‑request API. In the test stage add a Test built package job using the registry.gitlab.com/sat-mtl/telepresence/node-switcher:ci image, sets NODE_ENV=development, changes to /opt/node-switcher, runs npm install and npm run test. In the deploy stage add a Build and Deploy clean image job that runs only on develop and master branches, builds the Docker image with tag "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}", logs in similarly, and pushes it, and a Publish Package job that runs only on master, writes the npm auth token to .npmrc with echo "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${NPM_TOKEN}" > .npmrc and publishes the package with npm publish.
